quiz.title: Algebra-1: Unit 12 Quiz 11
quiz.subtitle: Key Features of Quadratic Functions
quiz.subject: Algebra 1
quiz.topic: Vertex, Axis of Symmetry, Intercepts, and Max/Min

questions.question: What is the vertex of the quadratic function $f(x) = x^2 - 6x + 5$?
What is the equation for the axis of symmetry of $f(x) = 2x^2 + 8x - 3$?
What are the x-intercepts of $f(x) = x^2 - 2x - 8$?
Does $f(x) = -3x^2 + 6x + 1$ have a maximum or minimum value, and why?
questions.explanation: When $a < 0$, the parabola opens downward and has a maximum value at the vertex.

questions.question: Given $f(x) = x^2 + 4x - 1$, what is the y-coordinate of the vertex?
What values of $x$ make $f(x) = 0$ for $f(x) = x^2 + 3x - 10$?
What is the y-intercept of $f(x) = 4x^2 - 8x + 5$?
questions.explanation: The constant term is 5, so the y-intercept is $(0, 5)$.

questions.question: What is the axis of symmetry for $f(x) = -x^2 + 10x - 21$?
Does $f(x) = \frac{1}{2}x^2 - 4x + 6$ have a maximum or minimum? What is the x-coordinate of the vertex?
questions.explanation: $a = \frac{1}{2} > 0$, so the parabola opens upward and has a minimum. $x = \frac{-b}{2a} = \frac{4}{1} = 4$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You can identify all key features of quadratic functions including vertex, axis of symmetry, intercepts, and direction of opening.
scoreMessages.70: Good work. Remember: $x = -b/(2a)$ gives the axis of symmetry. Substitute that value to find the vertex. The sign of $a$ tells you max ($a < 0$) or min ($a > 0$).
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing. Focus on: 1) Finding $x = -b/(2a)$ correctly, 2) Plugging that $x$ back into the function for the y-coordinate, 3) Factoring to find x-intercepts.
scoreMessages.0: Start by: 1) Identifying $a$, $b$, and $c$, 2) Finding the axis of symmetry $x = -b/(2a)$, 3) Finding the vertex by evaluating $f$ at that $x$, 4) Finding the y-intercept at $(0, c)$, 5) Solving $f(x) = 0$ for x-intercepts.
